article_title: Alaska Secures First Federal Funding to Bring ShakeAlert Earthquake Early Warning System North

content_preview.title: Alaska Secures First Federal Funding to Bring ShakeAlert Earthquake Early Warning System North
content_preview.summary: Congress allocates $2 million from the USGS ShakeAlert budget to begin building Alaska's first earthquake early warning system, targeting 90 percent of the state's population.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Congress has allocated $2 million to begin extending the ShakeAlert earthquake early warning system to Alaska, marking the first time federal funds have been directed toward bringing the technology to the most seismically active state in the United States. The allocation, drawn from a $34.9 million USGS ShakeAlert budget, will fund the initial implementation steps of a system that currently operates only in California, Oregon, and Washington, where it serves over 50 million resident...
